# UC-17.3.66 · Forcepoint Email Security DLP and Policy Blocks (KV SIEM Format)

## Description

Forcepoint Email Security emits structured SIEM events when messages violate DLP, encryption, or acceptable-use policies. Monitoring blocked or redirected mail by sender, recipient, and policy supports insider risk and regulatory use cases.

## Value

Extends DLP monitoring to the primary channel employees still use for high-volume data transfer—corporate email.

## Implementation

Enable SIEM integration in Forcepoint Security Manager using syslog key-value format compatible with Splunk. Confirm field names against your appliance’s SIEM map (suser, duser, exceptionReason). Restrict index access to mail security and compliance roles. Integrate with phishing and BEC workflows when `reason` indicates impersonation.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="forcepoint:email:kv" earliest=-7d
| eval pol=coalesce(cs_policy, deviceProcessName, policy)
| eval reason=coalesce(exceptionReason, cs_reason, reason)
| where match(lower(reason),"(?i)dlp|confidential|encrypt|block|violation")
| stats count by suser, duser, pol, reason
| sort -count
| head 40
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.Email
  by Email.user, Email.action, Email.src, Email.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (sender, recipient, policy), Timeline (block volume), Pie chart (policy mix).

## References

- [Splunk Connect for Syslog — Forcepoint Email Security](https://splunk.github.io/splunk-connect-for-syslog/main/sources/vendor/Forcepoint/)
